name: WOOVO Phuket Patong – by Swandor
detail: Good located in a side street at patong, only a 10 minutes walk to the beach. Nice view to the ocean from the 8th floor. The rooms were big and clean, modern and new. Would really recommend this hotel.

name: Crest Resort & Pool Villas
detail: When we drove up to this resort, I was thinking wow this is an amazing property, wish it's where we are staying. Then I saw the sign and we all got excited. It has some serious curb appeal. We drove up, parked our car and saw a lot of construction still going on. Security called for 2 golf carts to come down and pick up our luggage and the 3 of us. We took a steep and dangerous ride, passengers in the back need to hang on for dear life. The reception desk is located outdoors under some of the architecture. A pool wraps around the front of some of the pool access rooms. It's beautiful, but in an Ikea sort of way. Our room, the sea view room, while somewhat stylish looks great with a wonderful view but that's just the surface. If you stay in the room, you will find that there's only about 2 outlets inside and 1 outlet on the balcony. The wifi signal was a bit weak but I speedtested it and it measured in at 5Mb up and down. The finishes in the room are pretty low quality but I've found that with a lot of Phuket hotels. The restroom facilities are bad as they give you pretty much no privacy. I wouldn't recommend it for a family stay. With only 2 outlets, you have to plug in the hair dryer outside of the restroom area. The doors are glass and frosted in some parts but lend very little privacy. The toilet room is separated from the shower but the door only goes part way up letting every little noise you make out into the living space. Water pressure was low on the rainfall showerhead. The bathroom counter hardly had any usable space, you better learn to live tight. Beds are super firm. There wasn't much consideration with regard to safety so you better watch your kids. The ride up to the property was dangerous as is the property itself. Every doorway has a ledge so the moment you step out, you are falling down and you can really hurt yourself if you're not careful. The pool is amazing, views of 2 beaches and it has a bar area. Some rooms will have pool access at thei

name: Niranapa Boutique Resort
detail: The restaurant served very good food with a lot of options.

name: Lub d Phuket Patong
detail: if you are going to PHUKET, I highly recommend that you stay at Lub d Phuket Patong Hotel. ⭐ Lub d Phuket Patong ⭐ This place is located in Sawatdirak Road Patong, a 4-minute walk to the beach. It is also surrounded by massage shops, restaurants, convenience stores, and tour services. (However, due to Covid19 the open places are limited in all areas of Patong Beach.) We stayed 6 nights in this hotel and we loved the experience! It's a great memory we will cherish. The best thing about this hotel is they have great facilities. 🍹bar 👙swimming pool 🥊 boxing ring 🏋️‍♀️gym 🥙restaurant 🏓 table tennis 🎮 game room 🥅 foosball table 💃 hammock area For the free breakfast package: You need to order it at the bar. They have a variety of choices and I always chose the bread with bacon, scrambled eggs, mushroom, tater tots. It's yummy! As for coffee, tea, water, and juice, they have it all by the sidebar. It's self-service, therefore you need to clean up as you go. The hotel also offers a Picnic by the beach and a Floating breakfast by the swimming pool. You need to book these activities at least a day in advance. As for the Picnic by the beach, the prices are: -B450 without drinks -B600 with drinks (a bottle of red or white wine) 🍷 (We booked it for Sunset however, the weather did not cooperate at that time. The staff was very helpful in making the most of a bad situation and we ended up having an indoor picnic. It was still a very special memory.) As for Floating breakfast, the prices are: -B490 without drinks -B790 with drinks (red or white wine)🍷 Both activities come with yummy food and a very romantic set-up. (You can double-check the prices with the reception, as they might vary) All the staff was courteous and friendly. The daily housekeeper did a great job of making our room tidy and clean. KUDOS to TEAM LUB D Patong! I will surely come back to experience your  Pool Party. ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

name: Saba Suites Hotel Patong Phuket
detail: Location is nearest to walkiing street just 2mins aways.

name: PeranaKan Boutique Hotel
detail: Peranakan Hotel is very newly open hotel (only 3 weeks). It's located in the center of Phuket town easily to get to the markets, restaurants, cafes and other attractions in Phuket old town. The room is very convenience : comfort bed, flat TV, clean shower room also have free shower gel and shampoo. The owner and the staffs are polite and willing to help if you have any difficulties. Recommend for travelers to stay here :)

name: Selina Serenity Rawai Phuket
detail: Unbeatable 5 star resort on Phuket. Booked a 1 bedroom sea view, but upon arrival found out they upgraded us to a 2 bedroom penthouse. 150m2 appartment with 2 bedrooms, 2 bathrooms, and 60m2 rooftop balcony. State of the art equiped with 3 lcd tv's, and 4 airconditioning units. Swimming pool great and restaurant excellent as well. The restaurant is relatively expensive, but that seems to be the trend for hotel-restaurants everywhere in Thailand. 2 minor points of criticism: - Each bed had 4 pillows, but all are of the same type. Rather hard and thick, while feathered pillows should be the standard. - The sea on this side of Phuket is not really made for swimming. Sand is very coarse and water shallow up to 300 meters from the shore. Keep in mind that these minor downsides are close to neglectable. We spend 1 month in Thailand, and stayed exclusively in 5 star accomodations, of which none came close to the Serenity Resort

name: The Memory at On On Hotel
detail: If you want to escape the typical Thailand Scene prevalent down in Patong and experience the quieter simpler side of Phuket this is the place to stay. It is in the old Phuket Town which is about 30 minutes from the popular tourist beach areas like Patong. The oldest hotel in Phuket Town is almost completely refurbished (a few rooms still being completed it seems). We stayed in a delux-room which is spacious and a good mix of clean simple design and modern amenities like LED TV, Mini Fridge (empty) and room-safe. The hotel has no restaurants but this is not a problem as a huge selection of well-established restaurants are within minutes of walking from the hotel. I highly recommend Suay and Rendez-vous both down the road in front of you as you walk out of the entrance. For breakfast you cant beat the Gallery cafe by Pinky straight out again and turn left at the next intersection - a block up on your right-side. If there are to be any slight criticisms of the hotel it would be that the pillows are not very good and the walls are thin so you can hear noises from your neighbours - though not too intrusively. This is not a party hotel so in general it is quiet and relaxed. Well-spoken and friendly staff go out of their way to assist. A little gem in the heart of the old town.

name: Hotel Clover Patong Phuket
detail: SURF provided great value considering the cleanliness and sweeping views of the ocean and city from the poolside. Would recommend to anyone looking for the best hotel in Phuket.

name: Ibis Phuket Patong
detail: Overall, it is a clean hotel with friendly/helpful staff.
